mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening and compresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annually while CPI inflation exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which generates positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 2% quarterly fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 70% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signal robust residential investment and construction sector activity, which boosts economic growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods when housing starts cross the 700k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases when payroll growth exceeds 150k and unemployment falls by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicate a restrictive monetary policy response that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations when both funds rate and CPI growth are rising simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production reflect contracting manufacturing activity and reduced business investment, which directly translates to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns fail to materialize in more than 70% of periods following a month-over-month decline in industrial production.
